attack_paths.name: Server → Internal Services
attack_paths.description: Multiple server misconfigs increase attack surface

patches.instructions:
1. Save this script as APP-COOKIE-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x APP-COOKIE-001.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: sudo ./APP-COOKIE-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the patch: sudo ./APP-COOKIE-001.sh --apply
6. The script will create backups, modify Nginx configuration, validate settings, and reload Nginx
For Flask: install flask-wtf and initialise CSRFProtect. For Django: ensure CsrfViewMiddleware is in MIDDLEWARE and {% csrf_token %} is in all POST forms.

1. Save this script as APP-ADMIN-001-patch.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x APP-ADMIN-001-patch.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: ./APP-ADMIN-001-patch.sh
4. Review the proposed changes carefully
5. Apply the patch: sudo ./APP-ADMIN-001-patch.sh --apply
6. Verify the configuration is included in your main nginx config
7. Test admin endpoints to ensure they are properly restricted
8. Update IP allow lists if needed for your specific environment
patches.verification: curl -I http://localhost/admin && nginx -t && systemctl status nginx

patches.instructions: For Flask: install Flask-Limiter and apply limits to sensitive routes. For production, use Redis as storage_uri instead of memory://. Nginx-level rate limiting is recommended as an additional layer.
patches.verification: for i in $(seq 1 20); do curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' https://your-app.com/api/; done

patches.instructions:
1. Make the script executable: chmod +x APP-PASS-001.sh
2. First run in dry-run mode to see what changes will be made: ./APP-PASS-001.sh
3. Review the proposed changes carefully
4. Apply the fix with: sudo ./APP-PASS-001.sh --apply
5. Test password policy with a test user or passwd command
6. Verify that weak passwords are rejected
patches.verification: pwscore <<< 'weakpass' || echo 'Password policy is active - weak passwords rejected'

patches.instructions:
1. Save this script to a file (e.g., fix-hsts.sh)
2. Make it executable: chmod +x fix-hsts.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: sudo ./fix-hsts.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./fix-hsts.sh --apply
6. Verify with: curl -I https://yourdomain.com | grep -i strict-transport
Add all four header lines to your nginx server block. Adjust the Content-Security-Policy if your app loads resources from external CDNs. Test with `nginx -t` before reloading.
Add these directives to your nginx HTTPS server block. After applying, verify with SSL Labs: https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/analyze.html?d=your-domain.com

1. Save this script as WS-SRV-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x WS-SRV-001.sh
3. Run dry-run first: sudo ./WS-SRV-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./WS-SRV-001.sh --apply
6. The script will backup the original config, apply the fix, test the config, and reload Nginx

1. Save this script to a file (e.g., fix-ws-limit-001.sh)
2. Make it executable: chmod +x fix-ws-limit-001.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: ./fix-ws-limit-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: ./fix-ws-limit-001.sh --apply
6. Verify nginx is running properly after the change

1. Save this script to a file (e.g., fix-ssh-root-login.sh)
2. Make it executable: chmod +x fix-ssh-root-login.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: sudo ./fix-ssh-root-login.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./fix-ssh-root-login.sh --apply
6. Verify SSH service is running and test login from another session before closing current session
patches.verification: sudo sshd -t && grep '^PermitRootLogin no' /etc/ssh/sshd_config && systemctl is-active ssh

patches.instructions:
1. Save the patch as HOST-FW-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x HOST-FW-001.sh
3. Run dry-run first: sudo ./HOST-FW-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./HOST-FW-001.sh --apply
6. Verify SSH access still works before closing current session
patches.verification: sudo ufw status verbose | grep -E '(Status: active|22/tcp|80/tcp|443/tcp)'

patches.instructions:
1. Save this script as HOST-SVC-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x HOST-SVC-001.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: sudo ./HOST-SVC-001.sh --dry-run
4. Review the proposed changes carefully
5. Apply the changes: sudo ./HOST-SVC-001.sh --apply
6. Verify nginx is still running and accessible
7. If issues occur, restore from backup using the commands shown in the script output

results.message: Application code change — add SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE to app config.
Application code change — install and configure CSRF middleware.
Application code change — add authentication to admin routes.
Application code change — install Flask-Limiter or similar.
Application code change — add password validation to registration.
